summary: Improved shiftability of the mpcoll assembly by replacing many hardcoded `.4byte` instruction encodings with normal PowerPC instructions that reference labels, and by adding `.set` aliases for mpcoll data and sdata2 labels relative to exported anchor symbols. The PR also adjusted mpcoll asserts in C to use a named global `lbl_804D3948` instead of the string literal `"0"`, matching asm references to that symbol. The author noted that offsets within the file were still hardcoded, so this was an incremental shiftability cleanup rather than full relocation independence.

key_files.role: Main assembly cleanup; added data/sdata2 `.set` aliases and converted many hardcoded `.4byte` instruction encodings into symbolic instructions.
Added `char lbl_804D3948[2] = "0";` and changed several `__assert(..., "0")` calls to use `lbl_804D3948`.
Exported anchor labels `lbl_803BD260` and `lbl_804D7F88` as globals so mpcoll assembly could define relative aliases against them.

what_changed: Added `.set mpcoll_data_start, lbl_803BD260 + 0x10` and aliases such as `lbl_803BD270` and `lbl_803BD2A8` in `asm/melee/mp/mpcoll.s`.
Added `.set mpcoll_sdata2_start, lbl_804D7F88 + 0x8` and aliases for sdata2 constants including `lbl_804D7F98`, `lbl_804D7FA4`, `lbl_804D7FC8`, and `flt_804D7FD8`.
Replaced many lines of the form `.4byte 0x... # ... lfs/tlis/addi` with the actual symbolic PowerPC instruction, for example `lfs f0, lbl_804D7FA4@sda21(r2)`.
Converted hardcoded instruction encodings for data references such as `lbl_803BD2A8@ha/@l`, `lbl_803BD270@ha/@l`, and `lbl_804D3948@sda21` into normal assembler syntax.
Marked `lbl_803BD260` and `lbl_804D7F88` global in `asm/melee/pl/pl_unknown_001.s` so they can be used as cross-file anchors.
Introduced `lbl_804D3948` as a C global initialized to `"0"` and used it in three mpcoll `__assert` calls instead of an inline string literal.
smart_moves: Used anchor-plus-offset `.set` aliases to preserve existing label names while reducing dependence on absolute encoded instruction words.
Kept symbolic labels such as `lbl_804D7FA4` and `flt_804D7FD8` rather than replacing them with raw addresses, making future assembly movement easier to reason about.
Exported only the needed anchor labels from `pl_unknown_001.s`, avoiding a larger data reorganization in this PR.
Moved the assert string `"0"` behind the named symbol `lbl_804D3948`, allowing asm references to use a relocatable sda21 label instead of an encoded immediate.
decomp_lessons: Hardcoded `.4byte` instruction encodings can block shiftability even when the comment shows the intended instruction; replacing them with real symbolic instructions is a useful cleanup step.
For still-undecompiled assembly, `.set` aliases based on stable anchor labels can bridge the gap between raw addresses and fully named data structures.
If assembly refers to a string or tiny data symbol through `@sda21`, the C side may need an explicit named global instead of relying on an anonymous string literal.
Cross-file anchor labels may need to be declared `.global` before another assembly file can express relative labels from them.
The PR body explicitly says offsets within the file are still hardcoded, so this pattern improves shiftability but does not eliminate all local offset coupling.
naming_conventions: Used `mpcoll_data_start` as a local anchor-style name for data labels derived from `lbl_803BD260 + 0x10`.
Used `mpcoll_sdata2_start` as a local anchor-style name for sdata2 labels derived from `lbl_804D7F88 + 0x8`.
Preserved existing decomp placeholder labels such as `lbl_803BD270`, `lbl_803BD2A8`, `lbl_804D7FA4`, and `flt_804D7FD8` while making their definitions relative.
Kept the `flt_` prefix for the known float label `flt_804D7FD8`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace `.4byte 0xC00285C4 # ... lfs f0, lbl_804D7FA4@sda21(r2)` with `lfs f0, lbl_804D7FA4@sda21(r2)` so the assembler owns relocation.
Replace hardcoded `lis`/`addi` encodings with symbolic `@ha` and `@l` references for labels such as `lbl_803BD2A8` and `lbl_803BD270`.
Define related data labels as offsets from an exported anchor when exact data ownership is not yet fully cleaned up.
Use a named C global for an assert message when the assembly needs to refer to that exact symbol through small-data addressing.
Declare anchor labels `.global` in neighboring assembly data files when another asm translation unit needs to reference them.

review_feedback.note: No issue comments or review comments were present in the slice; no detailed reviewer rationale was available.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully decompile or further shift `mpcoll.s` after PR 609.
Search for remaining `.4byte 0x... #` instruction encodings in `asm/melee/mp/mpcoll.s`.
Search for additional uses of `mpcoll_data_start` and `mpcoll_sdata2_start` to see whether these anchors persisted.
Check whether `lbl_804D3948` was later renamed from a placeholder assert string symbol.
Look for other PRs that convert hardcoded instruction words into symbolic `@sda21`, `@ha`, and `@l` references.
